anti_patterns.name: separate_agent_site_path
anti_patterns.description: Avoid maintaining a parallel agent-only site (site/agent/, site/ai/ etc.). One canonical site benefits both humans and agents.

anti_patterns.name: aria_overuse
anti_patterns.description: role='button' on a non-<button> element when a native <button> would suffice (WebAIM: ARIA-heavy sites score worse on a11y).

anti_patterns.name: jsonld_html_divergence
anti_patterns.description: Schema.org JSON-LD price/availability must match visible HTML body (search-engine penalty + agent-trust loss).

anti_patterns.name: captcha_on_api
anti_patterns.description: API endpoints must not require CAPTCHA. Use scoped tokens + rate limits instead.

anti_patterns.name: vague_mcp_descriptions
anti_patterns.description: MCP tool-level description (function docstring of @mcp.tool / @server.tool) must be specific and >= 50 chars; vague placeholders like 'データを取得' are AX-hostile. Parameter Field descriptions are out of scope.

anti_patterns.name: browser_only_oauth
anti_patterns.description: Must offer at least one non-browser auth path (API token / device-code / client-credentials).

anti_patterns.name: server_side_session_state
anti_patterns.description: Server-side session-ID cookie state is hard for agent HTTP clients. Prefer stateless token auth.

anti_patterns.name: js_required_content
anti_patterns.description: Initial HTML must carry main content; retrieval crawlers do not execute JS. Hydrate, do not bootstrap.

anti_patterns.name: partially_humanized_ai
anti_patterns.description: TOAST research: partially-humanized AI is least trusted. Be clearly AI, no mascot / persona / first-person voice.
